title: MongoDB Schema Validation - Complete Guide
description: Comprehensive guide on implementing schema validation rules in MongoDB

schema_validation_overview.definition: Schema validation enforces rules on documents to ensure data quality and consistency
schema_validation_overview.flexibility: MongoDB is schema-less but supports optional schema validation

section_1_json_schema_basics.title: JSON Schema Validation Basics
section_1_json_schema_basics.definition: JSON Schema is a standard format for describing JSON document structure

key_properties.bsonType: MongoDB's extension - actual BSON type (string, int, object, array, etc.)

basic_example.description: Create students collection with basic validation
basic_example.what_it_does: Students collection requires studentId, name, age

example_1_required_fields.title: Enforce Required Fields
example_1_required_fields.validation_rule: Every document MUST have: studentId, name, age, major
example_1_required_fields.insert_success: db.students.insertOne({ studentId: '20260001', name: { first: 'Daksh', last: 'Chauhan' }, age: 20, major: 'Data Science' })
example_1_required_fields.insert_failure:
db.students.insertOne({ name: { first: 'Daksh' }, age: 20 })
// Error: Document missing required field 'studentId'

example_2_type_validation.title: Enforce Field Types
example_2_type_validation.insert_success: db.students.insertOne({ studentId: '20260001', age: 20, gpa: 3.85, isInternational: false })
example_2_type_validation.insert_failure:
db.students.insertOne({ studentId: 20260001 })
// Error: studentId must be string, not number

example_3_range_validation.title: Validate Number Ranges
example_3_range_validation.insert_failure:
db.students.insertOne({ age: 200, gpa: 5.0 })
// Error: age exceeds maximum 80, gpa exceeds maximum 4.0
example_3_range_validation.use_case: Prevent out-of-range values, business logic constraints

example_4_string_pattern.title: Validate String Patterns (Regex)
validation_rules: major must be one of: Data Science, CS, Engineering, Business
example_4_string_pattern.insert_failure:
db.students.insertOne({ studentId: 'ABC123', major: 'Medicine' })
// Error: studentId doesn't match pattern, major not in enum list

example_5_nested_object_validation.title: Validate Nested Objects
validation_rules: name must be object with first and last (both required)
example_5_nested_object_validation.insert_success: db.students.insertOne({ name: { first: 'Daksh', last: 'Chauhan' }, scholarshipDetails: { amount: 15000, awarded: true } })
example_5_nested_object_validation.insert_failure:
db.students.insertOne({ name: { first: 'Daksh' } })
// Error: name missing required field 'last'

example_6_array_validation.title: Validate Arrays
validation_rules: skills must be array with 1-10 string elements
courses can be any array of strings
example_6_array_validation.insert_failure:
db.students.insertOne({ skills: [] })
// Error: skills array has 0 items, minimum is 1
example_6_array_validation.use_case: Array size limits, element type checking

section_3_validation_actions.title: Validation Actions (error vs warn)
section_3_validation_actions.overview: Control what happens when validation fails

validationAction_warn.name: Validation Action: WARN
validationAction_warn.behavior: Allow invalid document but log warning

example.benefit1: See what would break without blocking

example_add_validation.situation: Collection exists without validation, add rules later

example_strict_validation.warning: May reject existing invalid documents on update

section_5_real_world_student_example.title: Complete Student Validation Example
section_5_real_world_student_example.description: Comprehensive validation for students collection (60 records)
validation_rules: Required: studentId, name, age, major, gpa
name: Object with required first/last (1-50 chars)

usecase_1_data_quality.title: Ensure Data Quality
usecase_1_data_quality.scenario: Prevent bad data from entering database
usecase_1_data_quality.validation: Required fields, type checking, range validation
usecase_1_data_quality.example: Validate GPA 0.0-4.0, age 16-80, required fields always present

usecase_2_api_safety.title: API Input Validation
usecase_2_api_safety.scenario: Validate data from REST API before storing
usecase_2_api_safety.benefit: Prevent client mistakes from corrupting database
usecase_2_api_safety.example: API sends studentId as number instead of string - validation catches it

usecase_3_business_rules.title: Enforce Business Rules
usecase_3_business_rules.scenario: Implement business logic at database level
usecase_3_business_rules.benefit: Rules enforced everywhere, not just in app
usecase_3_business_rules.example: GPA must be 0.0-4.0, status in (active, pending, graduated)

usecase_4_compliance.title: Regulatory Compliance
usecase_4_compliance.example: Ensure sensitive fields present, validate data formats

usecase_5_team_collaboration.title: Document Schema for Team
usecase_5_team_collaboration.scenario: Multiple developers working on same database
usecase_5_team_collaboration.benefit: Everyone knows required fields, types, ranges
usecase_5_team_collaboration.example: New developer sees validation rules, knows exactly what fields to provide

usecase_6_migration.title: Safe Schema Migration
usecase_6_migration.validation: Start with warn, move to error
usecase_6_migration.benefit: Catch issues before they break application
usecase_6_migration.example: Add new required field 'major' - use warn first, then error

practice_1_start_simple.then: Add type checking, ranges as needed

practice_2_document_schema.example: "minimum: 16, description: 'Must be at least 16 years old'"

practice_3_test_migrations.practice: Test validation changes in staging first
practice_3_test_migrations.process: 1. Apply to staging, 2. Test with existing data, 3. Apply to production

practice_4_use_warn_first.why: See what would break without blocking
practice_4_use_warn_first.timeline: Warn for 1-2 weeks, then switch to error

practice_5_handle_optional.practice: Decide which fields are truly required

practice_6_validate_sensitive.example: Email format, phone format, ID patterns

complex_validation.example: 10 properties, multiple nested objects, regex patterns
complex_validation.note: Still negligible for most use cases

section_8_performance_implications.benefit_vs_cost: Prevention of bad data far outweighs minimal performance cost

mistake_1_over_validation.problem: Makes inserts inflexible, breaks partial updates

mistake_2_wrong_bsontype.note: Common error - must use bsonType not type

mistake_3_no_flexibility.solution: Only validate specific fields, allow others

mistake_5_breaking_change.mistake: Applying strict validation to existing data
mistake_5_breaking_change.problem: Existing invalid documents can't be updated
mistake_5_breaking_change.solution: Use validationAction: 'warn' first, fix data, then switch to 'error'

operator_required.note: Field can be null if present

operator_enum.meaning: Field must be one of these values

operator_minmax_length.meaning: String length must be in range

operator_minmax_items.meaning: Array must have items in range

summary.definition: Schema validation enforces rules on MongoDB documents
summary.best_practice: Start simple with required fields, add complexity gradually
summary.when_to_use: Production environments, shared databases, compliance needs
